celebration: 🎉🎉 WORD NUMBER 1,000 lands this week — a thousand decodable words since the very first 'a'. A gold thousand-word ribbon is tied across the Pattern Tree, and the oi/oy leaf goes up beside it.

songs.title: Oy-Oy-Boy!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(oy… oy… oy…)
The happy sound!

[Hook]
Oy! Oy! Oy-oy-oy!
oi or oy — ONE sound!
Oy-oy-boy! Oy-oy-boy!
It's a boy! (a boy!)

[Verse 2]
Oy! Oy! Oy-oy-oy!
A coin! A toy!
Oil in the soil!
So much joy! (joy! joy!)

[Verse 3]
Oy! Oy! Oy-oy-oy!
Boil! Coil! Point!
Enjoy the toy!
(oy! oy! oy!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not oy!
(oy… oy… oy…)

[Outro]
oi — coin… oy — boy… (oy… oy…)
Joy! (a boy!)
(oy… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: oi/oy is the 'ahoy!' diphthong /ɔɪ/ — glides from 'aw' to 'ee', a bright bouncy sound. The whole family is small and cheerful; keep it playful. Placement hunch (verse hint): oy at the end (boy, toy, joy), oi in the middle (coin, oil, point). 'oy' sung as the diphthong, never letter names. Potato bridge intact.

songs.title: The Boy and the Coin
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The boy and the coin!
The boy and the coin!
Where is the coin? (where? where?)
Down in the soil!

[Verse 2]
The boy is Sam.
Sam sees a coin.
Sam digs in the soil! (dig! dig!)
Sam has the coin!

[Verse 3]
The coin is for a toy!
Sam has so much joy!
Boil? Coil? Point? (no! no!)
A coin! A coin!

[Outro]
The soil… the coin… (shhh…)
What is in the soil?!
(POTATO!)
The boy and the coin. (that's a thousand words!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The boy and the coin.' — Sam (the peg-doll boy) digs a lost coin out of the soil for a toy. 🎉 This week the class crosses ONE THOUSAND decodable words; the outro names it ('that's a thousand words!'). Let the choir cheer. Whisper before the potato; the coin was buried by the potato all along.

spreads.artNote: Sam — the peg-doll boy in a red shirt — standing in the spotlight, hands on hips, ready. Our oldest friend, back in the lead.

spreads.text: Sam sees a coin in the soil.
spreads.artNote: A glint of gold half-buried in dark soil; Sam crouched, pointing, eyes wide.
Sam holding the little gold coin up to the light, triumphant, soil on his hands.

spreads.text: The coin is for a toy!
spreads.artNote: Sam imagining — the coin above, a bright toy glowing beyond it, a thought-bubble of desire.
Sam mid-leap, coin clutched, pure delight. Segina claps at the edge of frame.

spreads.text: Boil? Coil? Point? No — a coin!
spreads.artNote: The oi drill made visible: little icons of a boil-pot, a coiled rope, a pencil point, all crossed out — and the coin, circled bright. The word-hunt joke.
Sam sitting happily with a spinning top toy, the coin traded away, contentment.

spreads.text: The coin is the best toy!
spreads.artNote: But look — Sam has kept the coin after all, holding it beside the toy, deciding the coin was the treasure. A quiet twist.
Back at the hole in the soil where the coin was: a single potato sits in it now, wearing a tiny miner's helmet. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Sam peeks over the edge.

sentences: The coin is in the soil.
The coin is the best toy.
extras: 🎉🎉 THE THOUSAND-WORD RIBBON: this week the cumulative decodable inventory crosses 1,000 words. Count it, name it, celebrate it — tie a gold ribbon across the whole Pattern Tree that reads 1,000. From one letter 'a' fifty-three weeks ago to a thousand words a child can read alone.

ONE SOUND, TWO SPELLINGS: oi vs oy — the last of the true vowel teams and the tidiest rule of all. oy lives at the END (boy, toy, joy, enjoy); oi lives in the MIDDLE (coin, oil, soil, point). Nearly no exceptions — let them feel the reliability as a reward after oo and ow.
Pattern Tree: the class reads coin/oil/boy/toy aloud and hangs the oi/oy leaf, right beside the thousand-word ribbon.
Moveable alphabet: build c-oi-n, b-oy, t-oy, s-oi-l, p-oi-n-t — testing the end/middle placement rule and finding it holds.

teacherFocus: oi/oy is the 'ahoy!' diphthong /ɔɪ/ — bright and bouncy, the smallest team and the friendliest. Best of all, its spelling rule actually holds: oy at the end (boy, toy, joy), oi in the middle (coin, oil, point). After the two-sound wobble of oo and ow, let this clean rule feel like a reward. 🎉🎉 And mark the milestone: this week the class crosses ONE THOUSAND decodable words. Count back to the first 'a', tie the gold thousand-word ribbon across the Pattern Tree, and let them feel the size of what they've built.
